name: Application Log Content
description: Application Log Content refers to logs generated by applications or services, providing a record of their activity. These logs may include metrics, errors, performance data, and operational alerts from web, mail, or other applications. These logs are vital for monitoring application behavior and detecting malicious activities or anomalies.

web_application_logs.description: Information about requests, responses, errors, and security events (e.g., unauthorized access attempts).

email_application_logs.description: Metadata about emails sent, received, or blocked (e.g., sender/receiver addresses, message IDs).

saas_application_logs.description: Activity logs including user logins, configuration changes, and access to sensitive resources.

cloud_application_logs.description: Control plane activities, including API calls, instance modifications, and network changes.

monitoring_logs.description: Insights into application performance, errors, and anomalies.

configure_application_logging.action: Enable logging within the application or service.

centralized_log_management.action: Use log management solutions or cloud-native logging solutions.
Configure the application to send logs to a centralized system for analysis.

cloud_specific_collection.action: Ensure logging is enabled for critical resources.

siem_integration.action: Integrate application logs with a SIEM platform for real-time correlation and analysis.
Use parsers to standardize log formats and extract key fields.

log_sources.Name: Application Log
